headline: Sabah Poverty Rate at 19.2% — Highest in Malaysia
context: DOSM's 2025 data confirmed Sabah's poverty rate at 19.2%, nearly 6 times the national average of 3.4%, with 680,000 Sabahans living below the poverty line despite the state's vast natural resource wealth.

cards.big: 680,000 Sabahans live in poverty at 19.2% — the state produces palm oil, gas, and timber but stays poor
cards.sub: Federal development spending per capita in Sabah is RM1,850 versus RM4,200 in Selangor

cards.big: Sabah contributes RM42 billion in petroleum and commodity exports but receives only RM6.8 billion in federal allocations
cards.sub: Net fiscal outflow of RM35 billion annually — Sabah subsidises Peninsula development more than it receives

cards.big: 42% of rural Sabah lacks paved road access — 180,000 children travel 2+ hours to reach nearest school
cards.sub: Stateless children estimated at 80,000-100,000 in Sabah — excluded from all government aid and education

cards.big: MA63 guaranteed Sabah 40% of revenue from the state — actual share has never exceeded 8% since 1974
cards.sub: Federal-state fiscal arrangement renegotiation promised by every PM since Najib remains unfulfilled

cards.big: Sabah's poverty in one of Malaysia's richest resource states isn't bad luck — it's 60 years of fiscal extraction without return
Should Sabah receive automatic 25% of all revenue generated from state resources as a constitutionally guaranteed minimum?
